analyst_notes: The new skill lifts pass rate from 79.2% → 95.8% (+16.7pp). Every old_skill failure tracks directly to one of the three iteration-2 changes: per-principle INVEST (all three evals), per-story What Changed (eval 1), or invented components (eval 1).

with_skill is ~18s slower per run and uses ~2000 more tokens on average — expected, since per-principle INVEST + per-story What Changed produce more output. Not a concerning tradeoff given the quality lift and that these runs are human-supervised authoring, not hot-path throughput.

The per-story 'What Changed' assertion is ambiguous under splits. The with_skill output shared a single block across 3a–3d daughter splits — the skill explicitly permits this, but the assertion was graded strictly. User feedback will determine whether this is actually a miss.
